skillName: Find the median from a set of numbers (incl midpoint using a calculator)
misconceptionSummary: Students do not know what to do when there is an even number of values.

steps.narration: With four values, the true middle falls in the gap between the second and third values. We use the average of those two to pin down that exact midpoint.
Add the two middle values and divide by two. This gives the point exactly halfway between them — which is where the median sits.
Notice that the extreme value — a hundred — has no effect on the median. It lands at the end of the sorted list and the middle value stays unchanged. That robustness is why the median is so useful.

visuals.label: Even count: average the two middle values

steps.narration: Four is the second value, eight is the third. Their midpoint is six — which is the median.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: stopping at two middle values without averaging them. Always add and halve to find the true midpoint.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Two middle values means you're not done yet. Add them and divide by two to find where the middle actually is.
